narration: In 2015, Earth felt spacetime ripple — and LIGO heard it.
visual_description: Hook visual: In 2015, Earth felt spacetime ripple — and LIGO heard it.
ai_image_prompt: Hook visual: In 2015, Earth felt spacetime ripple — and LIGO heard it.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Hook visual: In 2015, Earth felt spacetime ripple — and LIGO heard it.

narration: These ripples are called gravitational waves, caused by massive cosmic collisions.
visual_description: Context visual: These ripples are called gravitational waves, caused by massive cosmic collisions.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: These ripples are called gravitational waves, caused by massive cosmic collisions.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: These ripples are called gravitational waves, caused by massive cosmic collisions.

narration: LIGO uses laser interferometry to detect them — a method that measures tiny changes in distance.
visual_description: Escalation visual: LIGO uses laser interferometry to detect them — a method that measures tiny changes in distance.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: LIGO uses laser interferometry to detect them — a method that measures tiny changes in distance.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: LIGO uses laser interferometry to detect them — a method that measures tiny changes in distance.

narration: The first detection was GW150914, a collision of black holes over a billion light-years away.
visual_description: Evidence visual: The first detection was GW150914, a collision of black holes over a billion light-years away.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: The first detection was GW150914, a collision of black holes over a billion light-years away.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: The first detection was GW150914, a collision of black holes over a billion light-years away.

narration: This incredible technology lets us hear the universe's violent symphonies.
